features.name: concatenation of sized and unsized integer literals assigned to explicit-width port
features.description: Code should include a bitwise concatenation of two integer literals where one literal carries no explicit width (width must be inferred from its value) and the result is assigned to a port whose declared width is at least as wide as the inferred concatenation width

features.name: FIRRTL cat with unwidthed literal producing over-estimated result width
features.description: Code should include a FIRRTL-style concatenation where one operand is an unsigned integer literal without an explicit bit-width declaration, forcing width inference from the literal's numeric value, and the inferred concatenation width is compared against a narrower destination port width

features.name: constant folding of concatenated literals into a single sized constant
features.description: Code should include an output port assigned the concatenation of two compile-time constant literals of known widths, where the tool is expected to constant-fold the expression into a single sized literal
